title: agrostis stolonifera subsp. albida
intro: Agrostis stolonifera subsp. albida, commonly known as white creeping bentgrass, is a subspecies of grass that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Agrostis stolonifera subsp. albida include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
